title: Regex Tester
metaDescription: Test and debug regular expressions against sample text instantly in your browser, with live match highlighting and capture group output.
shortDescription: Test a regex pattern against sample text with live match highlighting.

guideSections.heading: Why the Global Flag Changes What exec() Returns
guideSections.body: Without the global flag, calling exec() repeatedly on the same pattern returns the same first match every time. With it, the regex object tracks a lastIndex position internally, advancing through the string on each call — which is exactly the behavior needed to find every match rather than just the first one.

guideSections.heading: Greedy Quantifiers Match More Than Most People Expect
guideSections.body: A pattern like <.+> against the text bold matches the entire string from the first < to the last >, not just , because + is greedy by default and grabs as much as possible before backtracking. Adding a ? after the quantifier, as in <.+?>, makes it lazy and matches the shortest possible string instead.
